STILL, William Henry Roland, L.D.S., R.C.S. (Eng.).—b. 1912; ed. Stationers’ Company’s Sch., Lond. Univ. and Lond. Hosp.; on war serv., R.N.V.R., 1939-45, sub. lieut. (S), actg. lieut (S); dent. surg., Falk. Is., 1937; dent. surg., Nig., 1941; lectr. in dent. surg. sch. of med., 1944; author of articles in tech. journals and of talk at R.C.S. on teeth and jaws of S. Nig., 1945.
STOBART, John William, A.M.I.A.E.—b. 1902; ed. Elswick Tech. Inst. and Atkinson Rd. Tech. Sch.; motor foreman, S.L., 1938; motor engr., 1942; man., rd. trans. dept., 1947.

STOCK, John Miles, M.Eng., M.I. Mech.E., M.I.E.E.—b. 1904; ed. Liscard High Sch., Wallasey, Cheshire and Liverpool Univ.; ch. elec. and mech. engr., P.W.D., Pal., 1938; senr. elec. engr., Nig., G.E.U., 1946; chmn., sub-comitee. on Art. XIII of Acre Conv. and responsible for taking over pub. utilities after Syrian campaign.

STOCKDALE, Sir Frank Arthur, G.C.M.G. (1945), K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1925).—b. 1883; ed. Wisbech Gram. Sch. and Magdalene Coll., Camb., M.A.; mycol. and lec. in agric. sci., imp. dept. of agric. for W.I., 1905; asst. dir., dept. of sci. and agric., and gov. botanist, B. Guiana, 1908; dir., agric., Maur., 1912; registr., co-op. cred. soc., 1913; official mem., coun. of gov., 1913; dir., agric., and registr., co-op. soc., Ceylon, 1916-29; M.L.C., Ceylon, 1921-29; mem., coun. Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921-29; mem., bd. of management tea research inst., and chmn. of exec. comitee, Ceylon rubber research scheme; agric. advsr. to S. of S., 1930-40; compt. for dev. and welf. in W.I., 1940-45; advr. on dev. planning, C.O., 1945; dep. chmn., col. dev. corpn., 1947; vice chmn., C.A.C.A.A.H., 1929-40; chmn., E.A. agric. confce., 1931; chmn., col. directors of agric. confces., 1931 and 1937; vice chmn., W.I. fruit and veg. confce., 1933; chmn., Imp. Inst. adv. coun. on plant and animal products, 1935-40; co-chmn. of Anglo-American Caribbean Coms., 1942-45; chmn., W.I. confce., 1944; travelled extensively; author of reports and articles relating to trop. agric. and co-operation; editor of Tropical Agriculturist, 1916-28.

STODDART, Alexander Frederick Richard.—b. 1904; ed. St. Peter's Sch., York; Loretto Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, Nig., 1927; asst. dist. offr., 1930; dist. offr., 1937; ch. asst. col. sec., S.L., 1942.

Sch., B.W.I., 1929; junr. educ. comsnr., W.I., 1936; educ. offr., Ken., 1938; admin. sec., educ. dept., 1943; senr. educ. offr., 1945.
STOKES, James Wallace, B.A., B.A.I.—b. 1908; ed. Drogheda Gram. Sch., Ireland and Dublin Univ.; on mil. serv. 1939-45, maj.; exec. engnr., P.W.D., Nig., 1936.

STONE, Richard Evelyn.—b. 1914; ed. Blundell's Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (2nd cl. hons. deg., hon. sch. of jurispr.); on mil. serv. 1940-46, maj.; cadet, Uga., 1937; asst. dist. offr., 1939; asst. dist. comsnr., Acholi dist. (prov. admin.).

STOREY, Harold Haydon, C.M.G. (1948), M.A., Ph.D., D.Sc., F.R.S.—b. 1894; ed. King Williams Coll., Isle of Man and Gonville and Caius Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1914-19; Union of S.A. 1922-28; E.A. Agric. Res. Inst., T.T., 1928-46; sec. for col. agric. research and sec., comttee, for col. agric., animal health and for. res., C.O., 1947; dep. dir., E.A. agric. and for. res. organ., 1948; author of various sci. papers.
STOREY, John Folds.—b. 1912; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; police constab., Pal., 1937; police sergt., 1939; inspr. of police, Br. Hond., 1941; asst. sup't., 1946.

STORRIER, Kathleen Wanless, M.B.E. (civ.).—b. —; ed. Glasgow Western Infirm., Rotten Row Hosp., Glasgow and Infant Welfare, Bradford; Q.A.I.M.N.S.I. (temp.), 1923-25; nursing sister, Nig., 1927; sent., 1939; Gam., 1939; G.C., 1943; sister tutor, nurses' training sch., Kumasi, 1944; matron, 1948.

STORRS, Sir Ronald, K.C.M.G. (1929), Kt. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (mil.) (1919), K.G. St. J., condeor. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece.—b. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (class. schol.), Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (1st cl. class. tripos, 1903); ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust., admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (desps.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem. Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comttee, Pal. exploration fund; mil. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and S. Pal.; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc., Jerusalem sch. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Pal. local govt. comsnr.; chmn., Pal. bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Pal. Oriental Soc'y; gov. and c-in-c, Cyp., 1926; do., N. Rhod., 1932; ret., 1934; rep. Cyp. at 17th cong. of orientalists, Oxford, 1928; rep. Cyp. at C.O. confere., 1930; joint editor, Handbook of Cyprus, 1930 edn.; author of Orientations, 1937, A Quarterly Record of the War, The First and Second Quarters, 1940, Drawing the R.A.F., 1942.

STOTT, Douglas Jeffrey, A.M.I.C.E.—b. 1896; ed. Royal High Sch., Edin. and Glasgow Tech. Coll.; on mil. serv. 1915-18; sub divsnl. offr., E. Bengal rlyw., 1926-29; senr. dist. engrnr., govt. rlyw., Jca., 1930; do., G.C., 1936; ch. engrnr., rlyw., G.C., 1946.

STOURTON, Ivor Herbert Evelyn Joseph, O.B.E. (Civ.) (1939).—b. 1901; ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; inspr., police, Maur., 1921; comsnr. of police, Berm., 1933; senr. supt. of police, Zanz., 1939; comsnr., police, Aden, 1940; Uga., 1945.

STOW, John Montague.—b. 1911; ed. Harrow Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Camb., B.A. (Cantab.) (hons.); admin. offr., Nig., 1934; secretariat, Gamb., 1938 ch. sec., Windward Is., 1944; adminstr., St. L., 1947.

STOWELL, Roger Faryon.—ed. Manchester Grum. Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (open schol.), B.A. (Oxon.), dip. anthrop. (Oxon.), dip. educ. (Lond.); on mil. serv. 1940-45, maj.; sup't., educ., T.T., 1930.

STOYLE, John Alexander Robertson, B.Sc. (hons.), A.R.I.C.—b. 1903; ed. Royal Belfast Acad. Inst. and Queen's Univ., Belfast (schol.); on mil. serv. 1941-43, capt.; gov. analyst, Maur., 1934; asst. gov. chem., Nig., 1938.

STRACHAN, Andrew Anderson, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.E.E., Chart.E.E.—b. 1896; ed. Stobswell Sec. Sch. and Univ. Coll., St. Andrews Univ.: on mil. serv., 1915-18 and 1941-46, maj.; asst. tel. engrnr., P. & T. dept., F.M.S., 1924; senr. engrnr., 1934; div. engrnr., 1938; contrlr., telecoms., postal serv., Mal., 1940; dir., telecoms., S'pore, 1946; ag. dir. gen., telecoms., Mal. & S'pore., 1948.

STRANGEWAYS, Thomas German.—b. 1907; ed. Camb. and County High Sch. and Trinity Hall, Camb., M.A. (Cantab.); inspr., plants and prod., G.C., 1931; agric. supt., 1937; agric. offr., N. Rhod., 1939; senr. agric. offr., H.K., 1948.

STRATHIE, Sir David Norman, K.C.I.E.—ed. Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., M.A. (hons.) and Balliol Coll., Oxford; on mil. serv. 1914-18 and. 1940-45; apptd., Indian C.S., 1911; comsnr. of inc. tax, Pal., 1941; India, 1942; fin. sec. and treas., Jca., 1946.

STRICKLAND, George Edward.—b. 1903; ed. Convent of the Sacred Heart, St. Julian's, Malta, Donai Sch., Woolhampton, Berks., Royal Univ. of Malta and Queen's Coll., Oxford, B.A., B.C.L. (Oxon.), 1st cl. hons. juris.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1932; on mil. serv. 1941-45, col.; crown coun., Nyasa., 1938; asst. legal advsr., Eritrea, 1941; dep. legal advsr., B.M.A., Tripolitania, 1943; legal advsr., B.M.A., Dodecanese, 1943; B.M.A., Tripolitania, 1944; attd. to H.K. planning unit, London, 1945; ch. legal advsr., civil affrs. unit, B.M.A., H.K., 1945; solr.-gen., 1947; pres., sessions ct. and ct. of appeal, Eritrea.
STRINGER, John Daniel.—b. 1914; ed. Uppingham Sch. and Camb. Univ., M.A. (Cantab.); admin. serv., Ken., 1936.

STRONG, Thomas Arthur, B.Sc. (for.) (Edin.).—b. 1899; ed. Stewart's Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; apptd. F.M.S., 1921; consvtr., forests, F.M.S., 1936; consvtr., forests and game warden, Ceylon, 1939; dir., forests, Mal., 1945; chmn. and mem. of various cttees. in Ceylon and Mal., del. to 5th Br. emp. for confce., author of statement on Forests of Malaya and the War.

STRONGE, Sir Herbert Cecil, Kt. (1930), K.C., Ireland.—b. 1875; ed. Falmouth Sch., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; prizes class. and English lit.; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bah., 1911; ch. just., Tonga, 1917; recd. thanks of Tongan govt. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comsn. in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925; Cyp., 1931; ret., 1938.

STUART, Charles Russell.—b. 1895; ed. Cranleigh; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; on mil. serv. 1915-19, capt. (desps.); mag., Uga., 1937; res. mag., 1940; chancellor, Uga. diocese; attd. leg. advsry. staff, C.O., 5th Nov., 1945-4th Jan., 1946; res. mag., Uga., 1946.

STUART, Marjory Jeanette.—b. 1906; ed. Convent of Immaculate Conception, Montreal, Central Br. Sch., H.K., Tech. Inst., H.K., and Moray House, Edinburgh; war service (nursing), 1941-42; pupil teacher, H.K., 1923; asst. mist., perm. staff., 1931; senr. mist., 1946.

STUART, William Hemming.—b. 1883; ed. Dale Coll., King William's Town, S.A., S. African Coll. Sch., (Cape Town medallist, B.A., 2nd in math. hons. exhibtn) and Worcester Coll., Oxford (B.A. juris. hons.); barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; advoc. sup. ct. of S.A.; on mil. serv. 1914-15, lieut.; chmn., Tongan defence coun., 1939-40; ch. just., Tonga, 31st Mar., 1938; 2nd puisne judge, B. TEMPANY, Sir Harold Augustin, Kt. (1946), C.M.G. (1941), C.B.E. (1933), D.Sc. (Lond.), F.R.I.C., F.C.S.—b. 1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., Lond.; asst. gov't. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; gov't. chemist and supt., agric., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; M.L.C., Antigua, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd internat. congress of trop. agric., 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., 1914; chmn. of trustees, Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905-16; dir., agric., Maur., 1917; nominated M.L.C., 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Maur. Inst., 1918; reported on S. African sugar indus., 1917; organised gov't. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agric. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prtn., Maur. Agric. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar indus., 1936; organising vice-pres., Maur., agric. and indus. exhibtn., 1926; chmn., various comtees.; dir., agric., S.S. and F.M.S., 1929; pres., bd., R.R.I., Mal., 1929; visited Java as del., 4th Pacific Sci. Cong., 1929; prtn., sch. of agric., Mal., in addn., 1930; chmn., comte., rice cult., Mal., 1930; del., confce. of col. dirs. of agric., Lond., 1931; dir. agric., S.S., and advsr. agric., M.S., 1935; asst. agric. advsr. to S. of S., 1936; agric. advsr., 1940-46; author of Principles of Tropical Agriculture and numerous papers and rept. on agric. chem., cane sugar production, agric. educ. and trop. agric.

TENNANT, Robert Allan, B.E. (N.U.I.), A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.C.E. (Ire).—b. 1901; ed. Galway Gram. Sch., Ireland and Nat. Univ. of Ireland; apptd. to Nig., 1928; exec. engr., N. Rhod., 1946; D.D.P.W., 1948.

Africa, 1920; ch. tel. engnr., Ken. and Uga., 1926; engnr.-in-ch., posts and tels., Ken., Uga. and T.T., 1933; examiner and moderator in tech. subjects for educ. dept., S. Sch. of Econ., 1935-41; war cab. off., 1941; Middle East Supply Centre, 1942; sec. staff, Anglo-American Comitee. of Inquiry on Palestine, 1946; sec., col. soc. sc. res. cl., 1946; sec., Liverpool hosp. comsn., 1933-34; jt. author of The Professions, 1932.

WINSTEADT, Sir Richard Olaf, K.B.E. (1935), C.M.G. (1926), M.A. (Oxon.), D. Litt (Oxon.).—b. 1878; ed. Magdalen Coll. Sch. and New Coll., Oxford, 2nd cl. mods.; 2nd cl. lit. hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., 1904; cl. V, 1910; sec. to comttee, for Malay studies in addn., 1913; cl. IV, 1913; federal examnr. in Malay in addn., 1913; cl. III, asst. dir., educ. S.S. and F.M.S., 1917; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, June-Aug., 1919, in addn.; cl. II, 1919; fed. examr. in Malay, S.S. and F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin., Raffles' Coll., Spore, 1921; offg. as sec. to high comsnr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addn., 1923; dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cl. IA, 1924; chmn., postal serv. comttee, in addn., chmn., tech. educn. comttee, in addn., 1925; rep., F.M.S., govt. at C.O. conf., Apr.-July 1927; chmn., comttee, med. research, S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., 1928; chmn., Malayan delegation to 4th Pacific serv. cong., Batavia, 1929; rep., F.M.S. at C.O. conf., 1930; gen. advsr., Johore, Oct., 1931; ret., 1935; mem., C.O. advsr. comttee, on educ., 1936-39; pres., assoc. of Br. WINSTON, Henry Perry.—b. 1884; clk., bailiff and interpreter, mag. ct., dist. "F.", Dominica, 1914; mag. dist. "F.", and coroner and gov. offr., eastern dist., 1938.

WINTERBOTTOM, John Miall, B.Sc., Ph.D. (Lond.).—b. 1903; ed. Ipswich Sch., (schol.) and Univ. Coll., Lond., pubns. edr. educ. dept., G.C., 1929; supt. native educ., N. Rhod., 1931; author of Understand the African (1946).
WISE, Norman, A.M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E.—b. 1899; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Sch., Stourbridge; on naval serv. 1915-22; stn. engnr., Nig., 1928; asst. elec. engnr., 1938; engnr., 1939.
WOLFF, John Arnold Harrop.—b. 1912; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Camb. Univ., B.A. (hons.) (Cantab.); cadet, Ken., 1935.

WOLFFSOHN, Arthur Norman, C.M.G. (1947), O.B.E. (1943).—b. 1888; ed. Dollar Acad.; on mil. serv., 1914-19, 2nd lieut.; gov. surv., B. Hond., 1922; dir., surv., 1935; col. sec., 1943; ag. gov., 1946, 1947; triang. surv., B. Hond., demarc. frontier line with Guatemala; chmn., comsn. of enq. into Stann Creek fire.
WOOD, Frank.—b. 1905; ed. King's Sch., Maclesfield; asst. postmr., Nyasa., 1930; postmr., 1934; senr., 1938; acctnt., 1943; asst. P.M.G., S.L., 1945.

WOOD, Harry, M.I.Loco.E.—b. 1895; on mil. serv., 1914-20, capt.; asst. loco. supt., K.U.R. & H., 1933; dist. loco. supt., 1938; loco. supt., 1938; asst. ch. mech. engr., loco supt., 1948; asst. ch. mech. engr., E.A.R. & H., 1948.
WOOD, Maxwell Goulter.—b. 1906; ed. King Edward's Sch., Bath and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Oxon.) (2nd cl. hons.) dip. educ. Oxon.; supt. educ., N.P Nig., 1929.

WOOD, Capt. Reginald Cecil Mervyn.—b. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. comdr., 1923; asst. treas., Ken., 1923; estab. offr., secretariat, Mar., 1928.

WOOD, Robert George, I.N.A.—b. 1906; ed. City of Lond. Sch. and H.M.S. "Worcester", mstr. mariner, Lt. cdtr., R.N.R. assoc.; served P. & O. Coy. and R.N., 1923-37; ch. exec. offr., port dept., Gib., 1938; asst. capt. of the port., 1942.
WOOD, Ronald Mackay, B.Sc. (civ. engnr.) (M/c.) A.M.I.C.E.—b. 1905; ed. Bradfield Coll., Glasgow Univ. and Manchester Univ.; on mil serv. 1941-45, capt. (interned); apptd., H.K., 1928.
WOOD, Walter Marsden.—b. 1914; ed. King Edward VII Sch., Lytham and Camb. Univ., B.A. (hons.) (Cantab.); asst. audr., Nig., 1935; educ. offr., 1937; admin. offr., 1946.

WOODGATE, George.—b. 1902; ed. Russell Hill Sch., Purley; on naval serv. 1919-23; police const., Portsmouth, 1924; police const., Ken., 1929; asst. inspr., police, Uga., 1937; inspr., police, 1940; asst. supt., 1945.
WOODHOUSE, Francis Moreton.—b. 1906; ed. Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Nig., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1932; dist. offr., 1939.

WOODING, John Conrad, B.A. (Durham), (class. hons.), M.A.—b. 1901; ed. Harrison Coll. and Codrington Coll., Barb., called to bar, Middle Temple; senr. asst. mstr., gram. sch., St. K., 1922; mem. of Middle Temple, 1933; ag. postmtr., St. K., 1935; regisr., and provost marshal, St. K. and addnl. mag., dists., C.D.H.K., 1935; mem. of pub. tenders comte., and inc. tax comsnr., 1936; mag., dist. D and addnl. mag., dist. C., St. K., 1939; mag., Antigua, 1940; inc. tax comsnt., Antigua, 1943; crown atty., St. L., 1943; ag. admntr., St. L., various periods during 1946-47; gov.'s dep., 10-14 and 22-26 Aug., 1947.

WOODMAN, John, O.B.E., B.A. (Oxon.).—b. 1888; ed. Royal Gram. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (Hulme schol.), hons. mods. and lit. hum.; on mil. serv., 1916-20, Lt.; apptd. civ. serv., Iraq., 1920-28; pres. ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of crim. appeal, Basrah, 1943-47; ch. just., Seychelles, 1945; ag. gov., 1947; puisne judge, N. Rhod., 1947, prac. as internat. lawyer, France, 1929-40; mem., internat. law soc. and acad. dip. internat. Paris, 1941-43; war dam. ins. dept., B/T.

WOODMAN, Raymond Henry, B.Sc. (Lond.), (dip. civ. & min. eng. (Lond.)).—b. 1903; ed. Wellington Sch., Somerset and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. serv. 1941-45, capt. (interned 1942-45); engr., P.W.D., H.K., 1929.

WOODROW, Frederick Henry, B.Sc. (Eng.), (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E.—b. 1905; ed. Mundella Sch. and Univ. Coll., Notts. (schol. and exhibtr.); asst. engr., P.W.D., Nig., 1929; assessor of values on ex-enemy prop. bldgs. in Br. Cams.

WOODS, Francis Joseph.—b. 1907; ed. Christian Brothers Coll., Bristol and Univ. of Bristol (1st cl. hons., geog.), Albert Fry prize best deg. in faculty, B.A.; on mil. serv. 1940-45, maj.; survr., N. Rhod., 1931.
WOODS, John.—b. 1897; ed. St. Paul's and King's Coll., Lond., B.A. (hons.), teach. dip.; on mil. serv. 1916-19, 2nd lieut.; asst. mstr., educ. dept., Ken., 1927; prin., Kitale Sch., 1935.

WOODS, Wilfred George, M.I.Loco.E.—b. 1899; ed. Tech. Coll., Crewe; artisan and tech. instr., T.T., 1922; foreman, wks. shops. and locos., 1928; draughtsman and instr., 1938; wks. man., rlwys., Trin., 1941; mech. engr., P.W.D., 1943; wks. man., rlwys., S.L., 1945.

WOODWARD, Denys Cuthbert, M.Inst.T.—b. 1902; ed. St. Peters Court, Broadstairs and Christ's Hosp., West Horsham; L.N.W.R., 1918-28; asst. traff. offr., rlwy., Nig., 1928; dist. traff. supt., 1938; senr., 1942; ch. traff. supt., 1945; gen. man., 1947.

WOODWARD, William, B.Sc. (eng., hons.), B'ham., A.M.I.C.E.—b.1892; ed. Nuneaton Gram. Sch., and Birmingham Univ.; on mil. serv. 1915-19 and 1941-45, maj.; engr., P.W.D., H.K., 1921; exec. engr., 1939; waterwks. engr., 1939.
WOKEY, Walter Keith Capel.—b. 1912; ed. Lewisham Sch., Weston; cadet (Chartered Co.'s serv.), N. Borneo, 1936; offr., cl. III., 1940; col. admin. serv., 1946.
WOKEY, Wilfred Gordon, B.A. (hons.) (Bristol).—b. 1915; ed. Newport High Sch., Bristol Univ.; on mil. serv. 1939-46, maj.; survr., Nig., 1937.

WOOL-LEWIS, Cyril Eric.—b. 1906; ed. Westminster Sch. and Trinity Coll. Camb., M.A. (Cantab.); on mil. serv. 1939-41, capt.; asst. comsnt. of police, S.L., 1935; asst. dist. comsnt., 1937; dist. comsnt., 1945; comsnt., Turks and Caicos Is., 1946.
WOOLFALL, Richard, A.M.I.E.E., A.M.Brit.I.R.E.—b. 1901; ed. Tiber Sch., Liverpool; tel. inspr., Ken., Uga. and T.T. posts and tels. dept., 1925; asst. engr., Ken., Uga. and T.T., 1945.

WOOLLEY, Sir Charles Campbell, K.C.M.G. (1943), C.M.G. (1937), O.B.E. (1934), M.C., K.J. St. J.—b. 1893; ed. Univ. Coll., Cardiff; on mil. serv., 1914-20 capt. (desps.); cadet, Ceylon, 1921; sec. to the gov., 1931; col. sec., J'ca., 1935; ch. sec., Nig., 1938; gov. and c.-in-c., Cyp. 1941; B. Guiana, 1947.
WOOLLEY, Ronald Wynne.—b. 1905; ed. Worcester Cathedral, King's Sch. and Christ's Coll., Camb., M.A.; cadet, G.C., 1927; dist. comsnt., 1937.
WORKMAN, Col. John Edward.—b. 1897; ed. privately; asst. comsnt., police, G.C., 1925; comsnt., police, Fiji, 1939; comndt., Fiji Defence Force, col., 1939; comsnt., police, N. Rhod., 1946.

WORLEY, Newnham Arthur.—b. 1892; ed. Reigate Gram. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb., B.A. (Cantab.), barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; mag. Ipoh, 1930; registr., sup. ct., F.M.S., 1931; dep. pub. proscr., S'pore, 1933; trans. to col. legal serv., 1937; solr.-gen. S.S., 1937; puisne judge, S.S., S'pore, 1941; ch. just., B. Guiana, 1947; chmn. pub. servs. contracts comsn., 1940-41; mem., comsn. on back pay for volunteers etc., 1946.

WORLEDGE, John Leonard, C.M.G. (1945).—b. 1895; ed. St. Paul's; on mil. serv., 1914-19 (desps.); asst. audr., Ken., 1920; asst. audr. in charge, Zanz., 1920-21; senr. asst. audr., Ken., 1925; seconded Seychelles, 1925-26 and 1929; audr., B. Guiana, 1932; audr. gen., J'ca., 1936; dep. dir., col. aud., 1941; dir. (later dir. gen.), col. aud., 1943.

WORMAL, William Gerald.—b. 1906; ed. Lincoln Sch. and Peterhouse, Camb., B.A. (hons.) (Cantab.), high stand. Yoruba, 1944; on mil. serv. 1940-46, lieut.-col.; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., 1931; seconded as labour offr., 1940-44; stats. offr., H.K., 1945; supplies offr., H.K. planning unit, C.O., 1945; sec. and chmn., to various comtees. in Nig. and H.K., 1932-46.
WORRELL, Cecil Theophilus Wilfred Edward.—b. 1894; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin., barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn; mag., Trin., 1933; asst. law offr., now first crown coun., 1936.
WORSLEY, Robert Lewkenor.—b. 1893; ed. Radley Coll. and S.E. Agric. Coll., Wye; on mil. serv., 1914-19, lieut.; Pal. Gendarmerie, 1922; comsnr., prisons, N. Rhod., 1942.
WORTHINGTON, Graham.—b. 1902; ed. St. Michael's Sch., Crewe; asst. govt. printer, S.L., 1928; supt., printing, G.C., 1933; govt. printer, S.L., 1939; G.C., 1943.
WREFORD, Charles Keith.—b. 1906; ed. Cranleigh and Clare Coll., Camb., B.A., barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., cl. III, 1931; cl. II, 1946.
WRENCH, Hector.—b. 1915; ed. Winchester Coll. and Oxford Univ., B.A. (Oxon.) (hons.); on mil. serv., 1939-43, capt.; admin. offr., cl. IV, Nig., 1939.

WRENSCH, Charles Gilfillan,—b. 1893; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch, Cape, St. Andrew's Coll. and Rhodes Univ. Coll., Grahamstown, S.A.; solr.; on mil. serv., 1914-20, capt.; cadet, Uga., 1926; asst. registr. of titles and conveyancer, Uga., 1933; admin-gen., off. recvr. and pub. trustee, Uga., 1938.
WRIGHT, Alec Michael John, A.R.I.B.A., P.A.S.I.—b. 1912; ed. Brentwood Sch.; on mil. serv., 1941-45, lieut. (p.o.w., H.K.); arch., P.W.D., H.K., 1938.

WRIGHT, Sir Andrew Barkworth, K.C.M.G. (1948), C.M.G. (1941), C.B.E. (1932), M.C. (bar).—b. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Camb. (schol.), M.A.; on mil. serv. 1914-19, maj., R. of O. 1940-42, lieut.-col. M.E. Forces; asst. sec., Cyp., 1922; ch. asst. sec., 1930; col. sec., 1937; ag. gov., 1938-39; col. sec., Trin., 1943; ag. gov., 1943-44-45-46; gov. and c.-in-c., Gam., 1947.

WRIGHT, Armine Charles Almroth.—b. 1910; ed. Sherborne Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford (schol.), M.A. (Oxon.), 2nd cl. hons. hist., dip. anthrop.; on mil. serv. 1940-44; cadet, Uga., 1933; dist. offr., 1935; sec. "A"; concerned in a peace settlement between Beni Amer and Hadendoa Tribes on Sudan, Nov., 1945-Mar., 1946; author of notes on tribal customs.
WRIGHT, Derick Bertram.—b. 1915; ed. Uppingham Sch., and Jesus Coll., Camb., B.A. (hons.) (Cantab.); on mil. serv. 1940-43, lieut.; cadet, Nig., 1937; p.s. and A.D.C. to gov.

WRIGHT, Frederick James, M.A., M.D., B.Chir. (Camb.), M.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., D.T.M. & H. (Eng.).—b. 1906; ed. Cooper's Company's Sch., Lond. (found ent. schol. and leav. exhib.), Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb. (exhib. and prizeman) and Lond. Hosp. (prizeman and res.); med. offr., Ken., 1936; med. spec., 1947; hon. sec., med. plan. comtee. of B.M.A., Ken., examiner in physiol., pharm., and med. at Makerere, Uga.; author of articles in E.A. Med. Journal and the Lancet.

WRIGHT, James, B.Sc. (hons.) A.I.C.T.A.—b. 1904; ed. Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrews Univ., Edin. Univ., East of Scotland Coll. of Agric. and I.C.T.A., 1st cl. hons. bot., medallist and exhib.; on mil. serv., 1939-42, capt.; mycologist, G.C., 1929; inspr., plants and produce, 1931; asst. supt., agric., 1933; supt., 1936; senr. agric. offr., 1945; Jca., 1946; served on war emerg. comtees., Ashanti, 1942-45; author of papers on agric. matters in sci. journals.

WRIGHT, John.—b. 1907; ed. New Plymouth Boys' High Sch., N.Z., Univ. of N.Z. (senr. Eng. schol.) and Oxford, M.A. (N.Z.), M.A. (Oxon.) mstr., Queen's Coll., Trin., 1934; lecturer in Eng., Yaba, Nig., 1947; compiled Q.R.C.-75 (a record of the "Queen's Royal College, 1870-1945").

WRIGHT, Robert Hepburn, F.R.G.S.—b. 1913; ed. Harrow Sch. (schol. and medallist) and Trinity Coll., Camb., M.A.; on war serv. 1943-46, F./Offrr.; cadet, Nig., 1936; asst. prin., C.O. (secondment), 1942; dist. offr., Nig., 1946; educ. offr., 1948; pres. of C.U. geog. club; p.s. and a.d.c. to gov., Nig., 1940-41; p.s. and a.d.c., gov., Cyp., 1942.
WRIGHT-NOOTH, George Allan Rodney.—b. 1917; cadet, police, H.K., 1938; asst. supt., 1942.

WYATT, Albert William.—b. 1900; ed. Leamington Sch. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; on mil. serv. 1919-26, capt.; interp., Luhaya; cadet, T.T., 1926; asst. dist. offr., 1928; dist. offr., 1938; dep. prov. comsnr., 1946.

WYATT, James Roger Godfrey.—b. 1895; ed. Worksop Coll., Notts.; mstr. mar. of foreign-going ships; on naval serv. 1914–18, 1940–45, p.o.w., H.K.; 1st boarding offr. and examntr. of mstr. and mates, 1926; ch., 1936; ship (nautical) survr. and examntr., mstr. and mates, 1940; survr., ships, hbr. dept., H.K., 1946; at times in charge of shipping offr.; control and upkeep of 50 large ships, moorings, etc.; inventor of improvements to sextants.

WYNNE-JONES, Edward Irvine.—b. 1895; ed. Felstead Sch. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, Univ. of Wales; on mil. serv. 1914–18, capt.; cadet, H.K., 1920; dist. offr., 1922; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1928; extra asst. col. sec., 1928; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1929; pol. mag., 1932 and 1934; P.M.G. and chmn., H.K. broadcasting comtce., 1936; chmn., overcrowding of prisons comtce., 1937; P.M.G., H.K., 1939.
YARNOLD, Douglas Henry, A.M.I.C.E.—b. 1908; ed. Cheltenham Gram. Sch., art. pupil to borough engnr., Cheltenham; on mil. serv. 1939–45, capt.; local govt. serv., U.K., 1930; apptd., Nig., 1937.
YATES, William Stewart.—b. 1905; ed. Coatham Sch. and Jesus Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.) (Oxon.), open exhib. mod. hist. (Oxon.); cadet, T.T., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1930; dist. offr., T.T., 1940.

YEARSLEY, Vincent Claude Ruskin.—b. 1891; ed. High Sch., Newcastle-u-Lyme; ent. C.A. off. after compet. examn., 1912; on mil. serv. 1914–19; asst. head, stores dept., 1928; Shipping dept., 1931; stores dept., 1934; dep. head, stores dept., 1938; head, stores dept., 1945.

YEO, Kok-Cheang, M.D., B.S. (H.K.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.) (distinct, Pts I and II).—b. 1903; ed. Hong Kong Univ., grad., M.B., B.S., L.S.H.T.M. and Camb. Univ.; Chinese health offr., 1928; lec. and exam. in pub. health, H.K. Univ., 1936; senr. Chinese health offr., 1939.

YOUNG, Andrew Hamilton, B.Sc. (agric.).—b. 1899; ed. George Watson’s Coll., Edin. and Edin. Univ.; on mil. serv. 1917–19, 2nd lieut.; inspr., produce, Nig., 1926; dep. suptg. inspr., 1927; suptg. inspr., 1928; dep. dir. of commerce and industry, 1945; dir., marketing and exports dept., 1948.

YOUNG, Major Sir Hubert Winthrop, K.C.M.G. (1934), Kt. (1932), C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—b. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; comsmd., R. Garrison Arty., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, army headqrtrs., Simla and N.W. Frontier, 1914–15 (1914–15 Star); asst. pol. offr., Mespot., 1915–17; dep. dir., local resources, Mespot., 1917–18 (ment. in desps.); gen. staff offr., 2nd gr., Hejaz operations, 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd cl. Order of Nadha); pres., local resources bd., Damascus, 1918; seconded to F.O., 1919; seconded to C.O., as asst. sec., 1921; accom. Mr. Churchill to Cairo conf., 1921; visited Pal. and Iraq on sp. serv., 1921–22; sp. serv., Lausanne for Turkish peace treaty, Jan., 1922 and at Brussels and Geneva for Iraq frontier dispute on various occasions during 1925 and 1926; ret. from army and apptd. col. sec., Gib., 1927; couns. to high comsnr., Iraq, 1929; ag. high comsnr., on four occasions; envoy extraordinary and min. plenipotentiary, Baghdad, Oct., 1932; gov. and c.in-c., Nyasa, 1932; N. Rhod., 1934; Trin., 1938–42; asst. sec., Relief dept., 1943–44; European regional offr., U.N.R.R.A., 1944–45; author of The Independent Arab, articles in T. E. Lawrence by his Friends, etc.

YOUNG, Sir Mark Aitchison, G.C.M.G. (1946), K.C.M.G. (1934), C.M.G. (1931).—b. 1886; ed. Eton and King’s Coll., Camb., B.A.; on mil. serv. 1915–19; cadet, Ceylon, 1909; off. asst. to govt. ag., N. Prov., 1910; do., Kandy, 1911; 4th asst. col. sec., 1913; 3rd asst. col. sec., 1914; resumed duty as 3rd asst. col. sec., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1920; asst. govt. ag., Hambantota, 1922; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. exec. coun., 1923; col. sec., S.L., 1928; ag. gov., 1929 and 1930; ch. sec., Pal., 1930; admstd. govt. for various periods, 1930–32; gov. and c.in-c., Barb., 1933; T.T., 1938; H.K., 1941; interned 1941–45; resumed duties, H.K., May, 1946 until May, 1947.

YOUNG, Stephen.—b. 1913; ed. St. Mary’s, Hexham, Northumberland; police const., Pal., 1937; asst. govt. printer, govt. printing and S.O., Pal., 1940; asst. supt., mach. shop, print. dept., Mal., 1948.
YOUNG, Sylvia Mary, B.Med., B.Surg. (Melbourne).—b. 1906; ed. Melbourne Church of England Girls’ Gram. Sch. and Melbourne Univ.; woman med. offr., Pal., 1935; S.L., 1944.

YOUNGER, William Maynard, M.B.E., F.R.G.S.—b. 1906; ed. Boy’s High Sch. and Transvaal Univ. Coll., Pretoria; on mil. serv. 1939–45, major; computing draughtsman, survey dept., N. Rhod., 1930; survr. and compr., 1938; survr., 1939.

YOUNGLAO, Leonard Paul, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (N.U.I.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.P.H. (J.H.U.).—b. 1901; ed. St. Mary’s Coll., Trinidad and Univ. Coll., Dublin (hons.), L.S.T.M. & H. and Johns Hopkins Univ. Sch. of Hygiene and Public Health, 1943; med. offr., Dominica, 1930; S.M.O. and M.O.H., 1945; B. Hond., 1948.
YU, Wan.—b. 1890; ed. St. Stephen’s Coll., H.K. and Oxford Univ., B.A. (Oxon.); inspr.-in-c., normal schls., H.K., 1920; inspr., vernacular schls. and sec., bd. of examiners, 1922.
